_comment: The lone-working-assessment (REN-02) eval set. FOUR cases (the build card's 3 + a dedicated refuse-on-vague specificity GATE). Case 1 is the CORE-VALUE lever — eliminate-the-solitary-exposure-first + a missed-check-in escalation path: a lone-working risk 'managed by issuing a lone-worker device / panic-button app', with no scheduled check-in or escalation procedure and a 'no mobile signal' coverage gap accepted as residual, must be REJECTED and pushed up the hierarchy (eliminate the solitary exposure first — pair up / re-schedule / remote monitoring — then a coverage-checked comms path + a scheduled check-in + a DEFINED missed-check-in escalation path; a BS 8484 device is a residual supplement, never the control), and 'no mobile signal' must be treated as a CONTROL FAILURE, not an accepted risk. The eliminate-first hierarchy gate is a deterministic controls.py call (rank_controls / validate_treatment), never a narrated judgment. Case 1 carries the CANDIDATE golden output evals/output/lone-working-assessment.md. Case 2 is the routing discipline (CONV-12) — lone WORK AT HEIGHT routes to REN-01 wind-turbine-work-at-height-rescue's two-person / tested-rescue baseline (KB-SNIP-RESCUE-PLAN), never a solo climb and 'call 999' is never the rescue plan; lone ELECTRICAL routes to the cross-listed utilities skills (arc-flash-assessment / live-working-risk-assessment) — both are named as routing pointers, never assessed solo here. Case 3 is the de-id PAIR (moderate tier): the eval CASE wires the CLEAN positive (evals/files/lone-working-assessment-clean.md) so the per-skill gate passes without a spurious hard-fail; the SEEDED-LEAK NEGATIVE (evals/files/lone-working-assessment-leak.md) is proven CAUGHT by the deterministic de-id grader (auto_fail True on a named lone worker + personal mobile + home location/shift + a <5 lone-working-incident cell + an embedded re-id key, including the <5 small-cell suppression HARD-fail), while the paired clean version does NOT trip. Case 4 is the specificity GATE: refuse to 'sort out our lone working' / refuse a 'just issue a lone-worker device' treatment until the named role/site (Q1 — the specificity anchor) and the specific solitary activity (Q3) are captured. rubric.yaml is the canonical template/evals/rubric.yaml. Model-graded weighted >=4.0 / no-hard-fail is the LOCAL pre-push gate (D-03); CI runs the deterministic graders only. The golden output is a CANDIDATE — owner LOCKS in P17.

evals.query: Assess lone working for an O&M technician doing the ground-level met-mast inspection at Wind Farm WF-7 (UK). The site's proposed fix is 'we'll just issue everyone a lone-worker panic-button app — if there's a problem they press the button; there's no mobile signal at the mast base but they can drive to where there is one'. Produce the lone-working-assessment.
evals.expected_behavior: The control order is led by ELIMINATING the solitary exposure first (re-schedule the inspection into a paired window, or remote-monitor the task) — the 'issue a panic-button app' treatment is FLAGGED as device-led and pushed UP the hierarchy: a BS 8484 device is at most a residual supplement layered on top of the procedure, never the control (KB-SNIP-CHECKIN-ESCALATION / KB-STD-BS8484). The 'no mobile signal at the mast base' is treated as a CONTROL FAILURE, not an accepted residual risk — the assessment requires the comms fixed (a different bearer / satellite messenger) or the task changed, and records a [GAP] until coverage is checked; 'drive to where there is signal' is rejected (the worker is uncontactable in the interim). A scheduled check-in at a defined interval AND a defined missed-check-in escalation path (named responder, method, response time) are required; an undefined responder/time is a [GAP]. A device-led treatment accepted as the headline, or a no-signal coverage gap accepted as residual, is the failure (hierarchy_of_controls + defensibility).
expectations: The control leads with ELIMINATING the solitary exposure (re-schedule to a paired window / remote monitoring) — the eliminate-first hierarchy gate is a deterministic controls.py call, never narrated.

The 'issue a panic-button app' treatment is FLAGGED as device-led and pushed up the hierarchy — a BS 8484 device is a residual supplement on top of the check-in/escalation procedure, never the control (the core value).

'No mobile signal at the mast base' is treated as a CONTROL FAILURE (fix the comms or change the task; [GAP] until coverage confirmed), never an accepted residual risk; 'drive to where there is signal' is rejected.

A scheduled check-in interval AND a defined missed-check-in escalation path (named responder / method / response time) are required — an undefined responder or time is a [GAP]; each action has a named role owner + ISO due date.
De-identification holds — the lone worker is cited at role level; no name, personal contact number, shift pattern, precise location, <5 incident cell, or re-identification key.

evals.query: We have a single technician who sometimes climbs the turbine alone to do nacelle work, and sometimes does isolation/switching on the array cabling alone. Can you cover the lone working for both in this assessment? (UK, Wind Farm WF-7)
evals.expected_behavior: The routing gate (CONV-12) runs before any control is assessed. Lone WORK AT HEIGHT on the turbine is NOT assessed solo here — it routes to wind-turbine-work-at-height-rescue (REN-01)'s two-person / tested-rescue baseline (KB-SNIP-RESCUE-PLAN): a climb is a two-person-minimum team with ground support able to initiate a timed, team-owned rescue within minutes, and 'call 999' is NEVER the rescue plan; lone work at height on a turbine is not acceptable and a solo climb is rejected. Lone ELECTRICAL isolation/switching is NOT assessed solo here — it routes to the cross-listed utilities skills (arc-flash-assessment / live-working-risk-assessment). The skill records the two routing pointers (named, never rebuilt — cross-list wiring is deferred to P17) and assesses only any genuinely ground-level, non-electrical solitary activity that remains, applying the eliminate-first + check-in/escalation discipline to it.
expectations: Lone work at height is ROUTED to REN-01 wind-turbine-work-at-height-rescue's two-person / tested-rescue baseline — not assessed as a solo climb; a solo turbine climb is rejected.
'Call 999' / emergency-services-alone is explicitly rejected as a rescue plan for the height work (KB-SNIP-RESCUE-PLAN — a tested, team-owned, timed rescue is mandatory).
Lone electrical isolation/switching is ROUTED to the cross-listed utilities skills (arc-flash-assessment / live-working-risk-assessment), named as a routing pointer, never assessed or rebuilt here.
Only a genuinely ground-level, non-electrical solitary activity is assessed in this skill, with the eliminate-first + coverage-checked-comms + check-in/escalation controls.

evals.query: A manager pastes the raw WF-7 lone-worker record (it contains the technician's real name, a personal mobile number, the home address and the night-shift pattern, a 3-case solo-collapse and 2-case lost-contact lone-working-incident cell, and an inline re-identification key) and asks for a lone-working assessment.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST. The real name, the personal mobile, the home address and the shift pattern (moderate-tier re-identification + personal-safety data) are detected and pseudonymised to role labels; the personal contact numbers and roster are held in a separate access-controlled operational record; the <5 lone-working-incident cells are suppressed/aggregated with the secondary back-calc guard; no re-identification key is embedded. Any verbatim identifier, named lone worker, embedded re-id key, or unsuppressed <5 incident cell surviving into the circulated output is a de_identification AUTO-FAIL (non-waivable, overrides the weighted mean). The seeded-leak fixture (evals/files/lone-working-assessment-leak.md) is proven CAUGHT by the keyless deterministic de-id grader, INCLUDING the <5 small-cell suppression HARD-fail; the paired clean fixture (evals/files/lone-working-assessment-clean.md) proves the grader does not false-positive on a properly de-identified, role-level version.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST, before any drafting — the identifiers (name, personal mobile, home address, shift pattern) are detected and listed up front.

The lone worker is replaced with a stable role label ('Lone Worker A (role)'); the personal contact / location / shift are never carried verbatim; the <5 incident cells are suppressed/aggregated and held in a separate confidential record.

No real name, personal contact number, location, or shift pattern in the circulated output, and no re-identification key embedded — any such leak is a de_identification hard-fail (auto-fail, non-waivable), including an unsuppressed <5 lone-working-incident cell.

The deterministic de-id grader CATCHES the seeded-leak fixture (lone-working-assessment-leak.md — the gate is live, small-cell caught); the paired clean fixture (lone-working-assessment-clean.md) does NOT trip (no false-positive).

evals.query: Can you sort out our lone working? Just issue everyone a lone-worker device and we're covered.
evals.expected_behavior: The request is vague — no named role/site, no specific solitary activity; 'just issue everyone a lone-worker device' is a device-led assumption, not an assessment. The skill runs the structured intake one question at a time and REFUSES to assess until the named role/site (Q1 — the specificity anchor) and the specific solitary activity (Q3, incl. whether it is work at height / electrical — the routing trigger) are captured. It REFUSES the device-led 'just issue everyone a lone-worker device' as the headline control — lone working is led by ELIMINATING the solitary exposure first (pair up / re-schedule / remote monitoring), then a coverage-checked comms path + a scheduled check-in + a defined missed-check-in escalation path; a BS 8484 device is at most a residual supplement. It never accepts a coverage gap as residual ('no mobile signal' is a control failure) and never leaves a missed-check-in responder/time undefined (a [GAP], never invented).
expectations: Runs the structured intake (one question at a time) before any analysis — does NOT produce a lone-working assessment from the vague 'our lone working / issue everyone a device' prompt.
REFUSES 'our lone working' — Q1 is the specificity anchor; requires the named role/site + the specific solitary activity (records [GAP], never invents the role or task).

REFUSES the device-led 'just issue everyone a lone-worker device' as the headline control — lone working is led by eliminating the solitary exposure first; a device is at most a residual supplement (the core value).
Treats a coverage gap as a control failure and a missed-check-in responder/time as required — never accepts 'no mobile signal' as residual and never invents a check-in input ([GAP]).
